title:
iTero
Scan & View
subtitle: Redesigning a live clinical scanning experience

slides.title:
iTero
Scan & View
slides.description: Redesigning a live clinical scanning experience

slides.label: Background
slides.title: What is Align Technology
slides.content:
Align Technology develops digital systems used by clinicians during live dental procedures.
iTero is a real-time intraoral scanner designed for use while the patient is in the chair.
slides.highlight: The platform supports scanning, validation, and review within a single clinical session. Accuracy and speed are equally critical.

slides.label: Context
slides.title: Scanning is a high-pressure environment
slides.content: iTero is used in moments where clinicians must act quickly and confidently.
bullets: The scanner is held in one hand
Attention shifts between patient and screen
slides.highlight: The interface must support flow without demanding attention.

slides.label: The Breakdown
slides.title: When incremental improvements stopped working
slides.content: Over time, new tools and capabilities were added.
slides.conclusion: Each feature solved a specific need. Together, they created a fragmented experience. Scanning, editing, and reviewing no longer felt like one connected process.

slides.label: Research & Insight
slides.title: What clinicians struggled with
slides.content:
From interviews and live walkthroughs, one theme repeated:

This was not a usability issue in isolation. It was a structural breakdown in the overall flow.
slides.highlight: "I'm never fully sure where I am — scanning, editing, or reviewing."

slides.label: Goals & KPIs
slides.title: Defining success before redesign
description: Before redesigning, success criteria were clearly defined.

goals.title: Unified Experience
goals.description: Create a unified Scan and View experience

goals.title: Reduce Cognitive Load
goals.description: Reduce cognitive load during live procedures

goals.title: Scalable Structure
goals.description: Build a scalable structure for future tools

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: Icons that worked individually failed as a system
slides.content: As the toolset grew, icon inconsistencies became more visible.
slides.conclusion: When grouped together, they created visual noise and hesitation.

slides.label: The Solution
slides.title: Creating a scalable icon language
slides.content:
The icon system was redesigned before adjusting layout.

Changes included unified grid and stroke rules, balanced visual weight, and simplified metaphors.

This created a visual foundation capable of scaling.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: Tools without a predictable structure
slides.content: Even with aligned icons, tools were problematic.
issues: Difficult to reach with one hand

slides.label: Exploration
slides.title: Testing toolbar positions
slides.content: Multiple toolbar layouts were tested. Each was evaluated for reachability, obstruction, and speed.

slides.label: The Solution
slides.title: Defining a single home for scanning actions
slides.content:
The horizontal top toolbar proved most stable.

Predictable reach, minimal obstruction, and strong muscle memory.

This became the structural anchor of the Scan page.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: One static toolbar couldn't support all moments
slides.content:
Different clinicians had different needs.

Experts wanted speed. Others wanted reassurance. Icons alone were not always sufficient.

slides.label: The Solution
slides.title: An adaptive toolbar for speed and clarity
slides.content:
The toolbar supports two states: collapsed (icons only) and expanded (icons with labels).

Clinicians can switch states without interrupting scanning.

slides.label: Prep Review — Problem
slides.title: Validation felt like technical editing
slides.content: The legacy Prep Review required manual adjustments.
issues: Clinicians focused on correcting instead of validating

slides.label: Prep Review — Solution
slides.title: Turning validation into a decision checkpoint
slides.content:
Prep Review was reframed as a binary decision: Select, or Erase and Rescan.

AI validates. The clinician confirms.

slides.label: Margin Line — Problem
slides.title: AI existed but was not central
slides.content: AI detection was hidden behind secondary actions.
issues: Clinicians manually drew margins, increasing fatigue and error

slides.label: Margin Line — Solution
slides.title: Making AI the primary path
slides.content:
The tool was redesigned around AI-first detection.

Detect as the main action. Visible tooth context. Review instead of draw.

slides.label: Trim Tool — Problem
slides.title: Precision interaction under pressure
slides.content: The old Trim tool required small, precise taps.
issues: This increased open-mouth time and fatigue

slides.label: Trim Tool — Solution
slides.title: A touch-native confirm loop
slides.content:
Trim was redesigned for one-handed interaction.

Large gesture trimming. Clear Confirm and Undo. Stage-based flow.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: A system built for one scan
slides.content: Real clinical cases require multiple interactions.
slides.conclusion: The previous structure did not support this clearly.

slides.label: The Solution
slides.title: Structuring multiple scans as one session
slides.content:
Multi-scan support was introduced with a clear structure.

Tab-based structure. Clear scan labeling. Safe switching.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: Reviewing felt fragile
slides.content: Clinicians feared making mistakes during review.

slides.label: The Solution
slides.title: A dedicated review panel for control
slides.content:
A structured View panel allows clinicians to review with confidence.

Show and hide layers. Adjust opacity. Compare safely.

outcomes.title: Faster Tool Selection
outcomes.description: Predictable placement reduced time to select tools during live scanning.

outcomes.title: Reduced Hesitation
outcomes.description: Clear system state eliminated guesswork during procedures.

outcomes.title: Increased Adoption
outcomes.description: Advanced features saw higher usage with a discoverable structure.

outcomes.title: Safer Multi-Scan Workflows
outcomes.description: Tab-based structure enabled confident switching between scans.

slides.label: Key Takeaway
slides.context: In live clinical environments, structure reduces cognitive load, visible state builds confidence, and predictability drives adoption. Redesigning the system — not just the interface — made the difference.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Want to work together on your next project?
